perturbation.original_text: "Business Day" means any day that is not a Saturday, a Sunday or other day on which banks are required or authorized by Law to be closed in Beijing.
perturbation.changed_text: "Business Day" means any day that is not a Saturday or a Sunday. Any other day on which banks are required or authorized by Law to be closed in Beijing will still be considered a business day.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding what constitutes a "Business Day." The original definition excludes days when banks are closed, while the modified definition includes them, creating uncertainty. This contradicts the subsequent sections, where the term "Business Day" is used to determine deadlines and obligations, such as notice periods or payment schedules. If a deadline falls on a day when banks are closed, it's unclear whether it's considered a Business Day for the purposes of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All notices, requests, claims, demands and other communications hereunder shall be in writing and shall be deemed duly given, made or received (i) on the date of delivery if delivered in person or by messenger service, (ii) on the date of confirmation of receipt of transmission by facsimile (or, the first (1s t) Business Day following such receipt if (a) such date of confirmation is not a Business Day or (b) confirmation of receipt is given after 5:00 p.m., Beijing time) or (iii) on the date of confirmation of receipt if delivered by an internationally recognized overnight courier service or registered or certified mail (or, the first (1s t) Business Day following such receipt if (a) such date of confirmation is not a Business Day or (b) confirmation of receipt is given after 5:00 p.m., Beijing time) to the respective parties hereto at the following addresses (or at such other address for a party as shall be specified in a notice given in accordance with this Section 10.3): if to Licensor: SINA Corporation 20/F Beijing Ideal International Plaza No. 58 Northwest 4th Ring Road Haidian District, Beijing, 100090 People's Republic of China Facsimile: +86 10 8260 7166 Attention: Head of Legal Department (Xie Guomin) with a copy (which shall not constitute notice) to: Shearman & Sterling LLP 12th Floor East Tower, Twin Towers B-12 Jianguomenwai Dajie Beijing 100022 People's Republic of China Facsimile: +86 10 6563 6001 Attention: Lee Edwards, Esq. if to Licensee: Beijing Yisheng Leju Information Services Co., Ltd. c/o CRIC Holdings Limited No. 383 Guangyan Road Shanghai 200072 People's Republic of China Facsimile: + 86 (21) 6086 7111 Attention: President with a copy (which shall not constitute notice) to: Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom 42/F, Edinburgh Tower, The Landmark 12 Queen's Road Central, Hong Kong Facsimile: +852 3740 4727 Attention: Jonathan B. Stone, Esq. and Z. Julie Gao, Esq.
perturbation.justification: The change to the definition of "Business Day" introduces a clear contradiction. The original text excludes days when banks are closed in Beijing from being considered Business Days. The changed text explicitly includes those days as Business Days. The contradicted text relies on the definition of "Business Day" to determine when notices are deemed received, specifically stating that if confirmation of receipt is not on a Business Day, then it is deemed received on the first Business Day following such receipt. Because the definition of Business Day has changed, this will create inconsistencies. It's now ambiguous how the contradicted text should be interpreted and enforced, thus creating a clear contradiction based on the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Licensor shall make available to Licensee the Licensed Content in substantially the same manner and with substantially the same speed and efficiency as such Licensed Content was made available to SINA Leju prior to the Effective Date, namely through Licensor's content database, but in no event with less speed, efficiency, or a lesser level of access than Licensor provides with respect to its own operations.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensor shall make available to Licensee the Licensed Content in a manner they deem fit. The speed and efficiency are solely at the Licensor's discretion and may not match what was previously provided to SINA Leju or what Licensor provides to its own operations.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding the level of service the Licensor must provide. Initially, it guarantees the same level of speed and efficiency as provided to SINA Leju, but now it states that this is at the Licensor's discretion. This contradicts Section 3.2 which specifies the standard of content distribution.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensor shall make available to Licensee the Licensed Content in a manner they deem fit. The speed and efficiency are solely at the Licensor's discretion and may not match what was previously provided to SINA Leju or what Licensor provides to its own operations. Licensee agrees to use the Licensed Domain Names only in accordance with such content distribution policy that Licensor uses in connection with its own business, and as may be established by Licensor and communicated in writing in advance to Licensee from time to time or as may otherwise be agreed to by the Parties from time to time, provided that Licensee shall be afforded the same period of time to implement any such content distribution policy as is afforded to Licensor's Affiliates and other third parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly contradicts itself. It states the Licensor shall make available the Licensed Content in a manner they deem fit, and that speed and efficiency are at the Licensor's discretion. The original text guarantees a level of service, while the new text removes this guarantee and puts it at the licensor's discretion. This creates a strong, clear in-text contradiction.
